title: Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum
intro: Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum, also known as Pseudoconnatum, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and creeping rhizome. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues.
benefits: Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum is a plant species known for its potential health benefits. Its leaves are traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. Additionally, Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum may have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. properties: Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum is reported to possess various therapeutic properties. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, where it is valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. constituents: Goniophlebium pseudoconnatum, like many other members of the Polypodiaceae family, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Research has also identified the presence of other active constituents such as terpenoids and saponins in this species.
